response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web engineer with genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core technical requirement of this role. The position demands modern C++ or Rust expertise as its foundation, and Bogdan's C++ exposure is limited to a brief six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — it does not appear in his skills summary, and no Rust experience is mentioned at all. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, and while his geospatial and distributed-systems background is directionally interesting, it is not oriented toward routing tile pipelines, map data transformation, or navigation guidance systems.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk on open geo-data platforms
Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, low-latency async I/O, real-time metadata pipelines)
Consistent Linux experience across multiple roles and personal projects, including systems-level work such as wave field synthesis with Jack audio
concerns: No meaningful C++ or Rust proficiency — the role's primary language requirement; C++ last used commercially in 2014–2015 for roughly six months
No routing tile pipeline, navigation guidance, or map data transformation experience; GIS work is data collection and display focused, not routing optimization
Skills section omits C++ and Rust entirely, signaling these are not active competencies
